sourceTitle: Get started with your Notion Agent

questions.question: How do you initially open the Notion Agent in your workspace?

options.text: Click the circular face icon in the bottom‑right corner or open the Notion AI tab in the left sidebar.
options.explanation: The article says you can launch the Agent by clicking the circular face icon in the bottom‑right or via the Notion AI tab.

options.text: Press Ctrl + Shift + A on any page.
options.explanation: There is no keyboard shortcut mentioned for opening the Agent.

options.text: Type "/agent" in a page block.
options.explanation: The slash command creates blocks, not the Agent interface.

options.text: Right‑click any block and select “Open Agent”.
options.explanation: The right‑click menu does not contain an “Open Agent” option.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct way to ask the Agent to create a new database?

options.text: “Create a marketing campaign database with tasks for 3 blog posts, 15 social posts, and 2 emails, include owner, due date, status columns, and timeline & board views.”
options.explanation: The example in the guide matches this phrasing and includes all required details.

options.text: “Make a table with three columns.”
options.explanation: That request is too vague; the Agent needs more specifics about purpose, properties, and views.

options.text: “Add a new page called Marketing.”
options.explanation: That creates a page, not a database with properties and views.

options.text: “Generate a spreadsheet in Google Sheets.”
options.explanation: The Agent works inside Notion, not external spreadsheet apps.

questions.question: When you want the Agent to edit an existing database view to show only your tasks for September, which part of the request is essential?

options.text: Specifying the filter criteria (e.g., tasks assigned to me and due in September).
options.explanation: Filters define what the view shows; the guide emphasizes being specific about filters.

options.text: Stating the background color you prefer for the view.
options.explanation: Color preferences are cosmetic and not required for the view to function.

options.text: Mentioning the type of chart you want.
options.explanation: A chart is optional and unrelated to a simple task list view.

options.text: Asking the Agent to rename the database.
options.explanation: Renaming the database is unrelated to creating a personalized view.

questions.question: What can the Notion Agent do with PDF files?

options.text: Extract structured data, create summaries, and build databases from the content.
options.explanation: The guide lists these exact capabilities for PDFs.

options.text: Convert the PDF into a video tutorial.
options.explanation: The Agent does not create videos from PDFs.

options.text: Edit the original PDF file directly.
options.explanation: The Agent works on Notion content, not on the PDF file itself.

options.text: Upload the PDF to Google Drive automatically.
options.explanation: Uploading to external services is not described as a PDF capability.

questions.question: Which statement best describes how the Agent handles multi‑step tasks?

options.text: You give a high‑level goal, and the Agent plans and executes each sub‑step end‑to‑end within Notion.
options.explanation: The article emphasizes that the Agent can complete multi‑step tasks end‑to‑end after you share your goals.

options.text: You must manually approve every intermediate step before the Agent proceeds.
options.explanation: The guide does not require manual approval for each step.

options.text: The Agent can only perform single‑action commands, not sequences.
options.explanation: Contrary to this, the Agent is described as capable of multi‑step workflows.

options.text: It only works on tasks that involve database creation, not page editing.
options.explanation: The Agent handles both database and page tasks, as described.

questions.question: If you want the Agent to update your company’s vacation policy page with new benefits, what is the correct way to phrase the request?

options.text: “Find our vacation policy page and add the new benefits while keeping the tone and formatting consistent.”
options.explanation: The guide provides a similar example and highlights that the Agent will locate and edit the page with consistent style.

options.text: “Create a new page called Vacation Benefits.”
options.explanation: This creates a new page rather than updating the existing policy page.

options.text: “Send an email to HR about the new benefits.”
options.explanation: The Agent does not send external emails in this context.

options.text: “Delete the old vacation policy page.”
options.explanation: Deletion is not part of the request; the Agent would confirm deletions but the goal is to edit, not delete.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a way the Agent can gather information across tools?

options.text: Scanning physical whiteboards in the office.
options.explanation: Physical scanning is not mentioned; the Agent works with digital content in Notion, Slack, Google Drive, and the web.

options.text: Pulling Slack conversations from a specific channel.
options.explanation: The guide cites Slack as a source the Agent can search.

options.text: Reading Google Drive files for research.
options.explanation: Google Drive is explicitly mentioned as a connected tool.

options.text: Fetching public web reviews for a feedback report.
options.explanation: The Agent can search the web for external reviews.

questions.question: How can you personalize the Agent’s communication style?

options.text: Create an instructions page that teaches the Agent your tone, workflows, and quirks.
options.explanation: The article explains that an instructions page can be used to set communication preferences.

options.text: Change the font size in the Notion settings.
options.explanation: Font size does not affect the Agent’s communication style.

options.text: Install a third‑party chatbot plugin.
options.explanation: Personalization is done within Notion AI, not via external plugins.

options.text: Set your operating system language to French.
options.explanation: System language does not directly customize the Agent’s behavior.

questions.question: What should you do if the Agent’s response isn’t exactly what you wanted?

options.text: Refine your request by telling the Agent what to change or add, and it will adjust accordingly.
options.explanation: The guide advises refining the prompt for better results.

options.text: Restart Notion and try again.
options.explanation: A restart isn’t required; you can simply clarify the request.

options.text: Contact Notion support for a manual fix.
options.explanation: While support is available, the immediate step is to clarify the prompt.

options.text: Disable the Agent and use the regular AI chat.
options.explanation: Disabling isn’t necessary; the Agent can be guided further.

questions.question: Which plan requirement must be met to unlock the Notion Agent?

options.text: Any plan that includes Notion AI.
options.explanation: The article states the Agent is available on any plan with Notion AI.

options.text: Only the Enterprise plan.
options.explanation: Enterprise is not the sole requirement; other AI‑enabled plans also work.

options.text: A separate paid add‑on called “Agent Pro”.
options.explanation: There is no separate add‑on mentioned.

options.text: A minimum of 10,000 blocks in your workspace.
options.explanation: Block count is unrelated to unlocking the Agent.
